spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (fetch(, window.) with runtime script (~3279 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (5 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (allowlist, blocked, fire, pass, requests, simulate, which).

edge_cases.rationale: Some failure / fallback handling shown (5 hits), but the demo skews toward the happy path and doesn't surface most edge cases the spec calls out.

references.rationale: References section cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link directly.

design_system.rationale: Button background overrides and status colors now set readable foreground colors using shared CSS variables.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Demo at least one explicit failure or edge case
openQuestions.detail: Spec / chromestatus motivation mentions failure modes worth showing — permission denial, missing-feature, empty input, malformed payload. Add a control or scripted scenario that walks the user through one and shows the resulting error or fallback.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Connection Allowlists is a feature designed to provide explicit control over external endpoints by restricting connections initiated via the Fetch API or other web platform APIs from a document or worker.  The proposed implementation involv") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://wicg.github.io/connection-allowlists to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~3279 chars of script across 5 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 3 pass / 3 partial across the six rubric criteria with no outright fails.
